name: Guthi of Rato Machindranath
description: Manages the annual Rato Machindranath Jatra chariot festival, one of the longest chariot processions in Nepal. Responsible for constructing the chariot and organizing the month-long festival.
managed_structures: Rato Machindranath Temple, Chariot construction yard
note: One of the oldest and most active Guthis in the Kathmandu Valley

name: Taleju Guthi
description: Manages the worship and rituals of Taleju Bhawani, the tutelary goddess of the Malla kings. Controls access to the inner sanctum which is restricted to specific priests.
note: Rituals are performed by hereditary Karmacharya priests

name: Nani Guthi of Bhaktapur
description: Musical Guthi responsible for organizing traditional Newar music during Bisket Jatra and other Bhaktapur festivals. Preserves traditional Dhime and Bhusyah drum ensembles.
managed_structures: Dattatreya Temple courtyard, Music practice halls
note: Trains young musicians in traditional Newar percussion

name: Pashupatinath Raj Guthi
description: Royal-endowed trust managing the Pashupatinath Temple complex, one of the most sacred Hindu sites in the world. Oversees cremation ghats, temple maintenance, and priestly activities.
managed_structures: Pashupatinath Temple, Arya Ghat, surrounding shrines
note: Receives state funding and manages vast temple landholdings

name: Si Guthi of Kwabahal
description: Funeral trust for the Kwabahal neighborhood in Patan. Ensures proper death rituals are performed for all member families regardless of economic status.
note: Members pay annual dues to ensure funeral rites are covered

name: Manka Guthi of Thimi
description: Agricultural trust managing communal farmlands in Thimi. Coordinates rice planting schedules, irrigation management, and the annual harvest festival.
